member.name: Mr Joshua Reynolds

addresses: Ground Floor Right-Hand Suite, Burlington House, York Road, Maidenhead, SL6 1SQ

member.synopsis: Mr Joshua Reynolds is the Liberal Democrat MP for Maidenhead , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Investment and Trade).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Vetting: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to help reduce delays in the processing of Disclosure and Barring Service check; and what assessment she has made of the impact that such delays have on individuals
writtenQuestions.answerText: As an Executive Non-Departmental Public Body of the Home Office, the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) is operationally independent and responsible for the service it provides.The DBS operates to service standards of completing 85% of Basic check appli...

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent discussions she has had with Ofcom on differences in pricing between new and existing broadband customers in areas served by a single provider.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government works closely with Ofcom to monitor consumer outcomes, and Ministers engage regularly with the regulator on a range of issues, including pricing and consumer protection. The new Telecoms Consumer Charter, published February 2026, includes i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Folic Acid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what is the evidence base for mandatory fortification of white flour with folic acid; what consideration has been given to potential risks associated with long term population-wide exposure; and if
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government laid legislation in England on 14 November 2024 to introduce the mandatory fortification of non-wholemeal wheat flour with folic acid. The policy is estimated to prevent approximately 200 babies being born with neural tube defects each year. Le...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Concessions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department issues guidance to local authorities on the eligibility criteria for disabled persons’ bus passes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Yes. The Department's guidance for Local Transport Authorities on assessing the eligibility of disabled people for concessionary bus travel is published on GOV.UK at: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/guidance-for-local-authorities-on-eligibility...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Each National Health Service provider is responsible for the procurement and operation of appointment booking systems, as well as communication with patients about their appointments. National guidance expects trusts to use the Digital Technology Assessme...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pathology: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that pathology test results can be transferred electronically between NHS pathology providers and GP practices that use different laboratory informatio
writtenQuestions.answerText: Historically, each National Health Service trust has had a separate Laboratory Information and Management System (LIMS), which precludes the seamless digital sharing of pathology results and reports between trusts.NHS England has provided funding and expe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his Department has issued guidance to local authorities and car park operators on the provision, location and accessibility of parent and child parking spaces in public c
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has not issued guidance on the provision, location or accessibility of parent and child parking spaces in public car parks and retail centres.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: Insurers make commercial decisions about the terms on which they will offer cover following an assessment of the relevant risks. This is usually informed by the insurer’s claims experience and other industry-wide statistics The Equality Act 2010 generally...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The provision and design of parking facilities in council‑owned car parks, including parent and child bays, is the responsibility of individual local authorities, which are best placed to determine local provision based on the needs of their communities.T...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the level of collision risk associated with drivers aged 70 and over compared with other age groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In Great Britain in 2024, around 24% of all car drivers killed were drivers aged 70 or older, with 12% of all casualties in car collisions being in collisions involving older drivers. The risk of being killed or seriously injured (KSI) per mile travelled ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Blood Tests: General Practitioners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of enabling patients to attend any NHS phlebotomy services for blood tests requested by their GP irrespective of the pathology
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on the organisation of phlebotomy and pathology services are made locally by integrated care boards and National Health Service providers, reflecting local clinical pathways, safety requirements, and operational arrangements. This includes ensur...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Homicide: Victim Support Schemes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what financial contribution her Department has made to the Victim Support Homicide Service in each of the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that families bereaved by homicide abroad receive the support they need. As part of this, the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office provides a contribution towards the overall £5.5 million funding of the Na...

writtenQuestions.heading: Victim Support Schemes: Disabled
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of the accessibility for disabled people of online information about the victims' services it funds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice provides online information about the services available to victims and witnesses on the victim and witness website (victimandwitnessinformation.org.uk). Whilst some parts of the website are not fully accessible, for example some i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Victims: Codes of Practice
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when his Department plans to publish its response to the consultation on a new Victims' Code.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The consultation on a new Victims’ Code closed on the 30 April. We are now taking the time to consider nearly 200 responses, and we will issue a public response in due course, ahead of finalising the new Code and bringing it into force.

writtenQuestions.heading: Victim Support Schemes: British Nationals Abroad
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what proportion of the funding allocated to victim support services for the three years from 2026 to 2029 has been apportioned to homicide of British Citizens abroad.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that families bereaved by homicide, including those bereaved by homicide abroad, receive the support they need to build resilience and cope with the impact of these devastating crimes. This is why we commission the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bereavement: Counselling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether his Department plans to increase the number of counselling sessions available to bereaved families through the Homicide Service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that families bereaved by homicide receive the support they need to help them build resilience to cope with the impact of these devastating crimes. That is why we commission a National Homicide Service, delivered by...

writtenQuestions.heading: Adoption
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she plans to issue a formal apology to those affected by historic forced adoptions in England; and if she will set out a timetable for making such an apology.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises that historical forced adoption practices caused deep and lasting harm to many mothers, adoptees and families, and we take those experiences extremely seriously.We accept that the state played a role in what happened, and the cas...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the government’s special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) reform proposals, education, health and care plans will be underpinned by clear evidence-based Specialist Provision Packages (SPPs). All providers approved to deliver SPPs will be re...
